Roku Outdoor Security Camera

Buy for $30 (Reg. $50)

What we love

If you’re looking for a cheaper alternative to bolster your home’s security, this Roku camera can do so without weighing on your finances. Although it is designed to handle outdoor installations with an IP65 weather-resistant construction, my neighbors actually have one positioned indoors in their window. Once installed, it’ll provide you with 1,080p HD full-color resolution video feeds (plus night vision) – plus, there’s motion and sound detection that alerts you through the companion app. What’s more, you can even pull up its feed onto your Roku TV or player with a simple press of a button.

Yesterday Nintendo hosted an IndieWorld showcase featuring a number of compelling upcoming indie tiles and even some that released shortly after the presentation. One of those titles was Blue Prince – a mysterious, “genre-defying experience, filled with a unique mix of mystery, strategy, and puzzles that weave together to create an unpredictable journey. Will your explorative steps lead you to the rumored Room 46?” It is now available for purchase on the eShop and with a nice launch deal too at $19.79, down 34% from the regular $30 list price. 

Nintendo announces new classic Mario games set for release on Mario Day 2026 next week

Nintendo officially announces 2026 international Mario Day Switch 2 console bundle

Mario Day 2026 is next week – What to expect, FREE $5 store credits confirmed, new game releases and hardware?

more…

The acquisition, which also includes the latter’s Downdetector outage tracker, costs the Irish company US$1.2 billion (~RM 4.73 billion). Specifically, the deal includes Speedtest, Downdetector, Ekahau, and RootMetrics. “Modern networks have evolved from simple […]
